pages.title: About – JEFF SMITH MP
pages.meta_description: I’m proud to be the Labour MP for Manchester Withington. I was born and raised in Withington, and it’s a privilege to represent the people of my local area in Parliament. I was elected to Parliament in 2015, and prior to that I served as a local Councillor in my home ward of Old Moat,…

h2: CONTACT JEFF
pages.text_excerpt: About – JEFF SMITH MP JEFF SMITH MP WORKING HARD FOR MANCHESTER WITHINGTON Menu Skip to content Home About Parliament PMQs & Parliamentary Tours Drug Law Reform Mental Health Environment Policies Assisted Dying Bill Israel, Palestine & Lebanon Help & Advice Cost of Living Crisis Cost of Living Crisis Energy Support Debt Advice Mortgage repayments Emergency Support Finances, Social Security & Employment Housing Immigration & Asylum Local Councillors Contact Privacy Policy Your Local Election Candidates for May 2026 Cllr Suzannah Reeves – Old Moat Ward Ben Williams – Didsbury West Ward Cllr Joanna Midgley – Chorlton Park Ward Cllr John Hacking – Chorlton Ward Cllr Andrew Simcock – Didsbury East Ward Cllr Angela Gartside – Withington Ward About I’m proud to be the Labour MP for Manchester Withington. I was born and raised in Withington, and it’s a privilege to represent the people of my local area in Parliament. I was elected to Parliament in 2015, and prior to that I served as a local Councillor in my home ward of Old Moat, becoming the Council’s Executive Member for Education, for Finance and for Housing & Regeneration. I saw how Tory budget cuts damaged local services but also how local government could provide innovative solutions when the government in Westminster cut back investment in our communities. Before becoming an MP, I had a career in event management and as a DJ in clubs and colleges across the north of England, seeing first-hand the important role music has played in Manchester’s history and culture. In Parliament, I served in the opposition whips office, as well as in Shadow Ministerial posts in the Local Government, Culture Media & Sport, and Energy & Net Zero teams. From July 2024 to September 2025, I served as a Senior Government Whip. As your MP, I have an office in the heart of the constituency, at Withington Fire Station, as well as one in Parliament. My team are always available to support residents with problems. I run advice surgeries by appointment, and I am always keen to visit local community groups, schools and social activities. If you’d like me to visit a local school or community organisation, please get in touch with my office. Away from politics, I’m a lifelong Manchester City season ticket holder and take every opportunity I can to see Bruce Springsteen in concert. Please don’t hesitate to get in touch with me with any questions, or for advice and support. You can email me at jeff.smith.mp@parliament.uk or phone our constituency office on 0161 445 0678. pages.text_excerpt: PMQs & Parliamentary Tours – JEFF SMITH MP JEFF SMITH MP WORKING HARD FOR MANCHESTER WITHINGTON Menu Skip to content Home About Parliament PMQs & Parliamentary Tours Drug Law Reform Mental Health Environment Policies Assisted Dying Bill Israel, Palestine & Lebanon Help & Advice Cost of Living Crisis Cost of Living Crisis Energy Support Debt Advice Mortgage repayments Emergency Support Finances, Social Security & Employment Housing Immigration & Asylum Local Councillors Contact Privacy Policy Your Local Election Candidates for May 2026 Cllr Suzannah Reeves – Old Moat Ward Ben Williams – Didsbury West Ward Cllr Joanna Midgley – Chorlton Park Ward Cllr John Hacking – Chorlton Ward Cllr Andrew Simcock – Didsbury East Ward Cllr Angela Gartside – Withington Ward PMQs & Parliamentary Tours Prime Minister’s Questions (PMQs) PMQs take place every Wednesday at 12:00 PM for 30 minutes. Constituents can apply for tickets through my office. We receive a very limited number of PMQ tickets each month, and they are in high demand, so it is advisable to book some months in advance. Requests are dealt with on a first-come, first-served basis, and will usually be for no more than two tickets per application. To request tickets, please provide the full names and addresses of individuals attending. UK Parliament Tours UK Parliament offers a range of guided tours, giving visitors the chance to explore the building and learn about the work of UK Parliament. Contact my office if you wish to book an Inside UK Parliament tour, which lasts 75 minutes and is free of charge and open to UK residents. The fascinating tour focuses on how Parliament operates, the role of MPs, and key historical and heritage aspects of the Palace of Westminster. Big Ben Tour MPs can book a free official tour of Big Ben on behalf of constituents who want to explore the famous Elizabeth Tower at Parliament. This guided tour takes you up a narrow 334-step spiral stairwell to the belfry where Big Ben, the Great Bell, hangs. You will see the inner workings of the clock mechanism, stand next to the world-famous Big Ben bell as it strikes the hour, and step behind the dials each spanning 6.9m in diameter. Please note, visitors are required to be aged 11 or over by the date of their visit and children aged 11-17 require adult supervision. MPs are only allocated 8 bookings each year. If you’d like to book a tour or PMQs tickets through my office, please contact: jeff.smith.mp@parlaiment.uk Share this: Share on X (Opens in new window) X Share on Facebook (Opens in new window) Facebook Like Loading... pages.text_excerpt: Help & Advice – JEFF SMITH MP JEFF SMITH MP WORKING HARD FOR MANCHESTER WITHINGTON Menu Skip to content Home About Parliament PMQs & Parliamentary Tours Drug Law Reform Mental Health Environment Policies Assisted Dying Bill Israel, Palestine & Lebanon Help & Advice Cost of Living Crisis Cost of Living Crisis Energy Support Debt Advice Mortgage repayments Emergency Support Finances, Social Security & Employment Housing Immigration & Asylum Local Councillors Contact Privacy Policy Your Local Election Candidates for May 2026 Cllr Suzannah Reeves – Old Moat Ward Ben Williams – Didsbury West Ward Cllr Joanna Midgley – Chorlton Park Ward Cllr John Hacking – Chorlton Ward Cllr Andrew Simcock – Didsbury East Ward Cllr Angela Gartside – Withington Ward Help & Advice What can Jeff do for you? Members of Parliament (MPs) are there to help with those matters for which Parliament or central government is responsible. For many matters that are more local, the appropriate first step would be to contact your local councillor. You can find your local councillor here . MPs cannot help in private disputes with neighbours, with an employer, with family matters or with companies who have sold you faulty goods; nor can they interfere with decisions made by the courts. However, Jeff will give help and advice where possible on these matters and work with your local councillor to help constituents whose problems relate to the services provided by local authorities such as dustbins, housing repairs or social services. MPs can help if your problems concern central government policies such as: The National Health Service HM Revenue and Customs (tax and benefits) The Department for Work and Pensions (benefits, pensions, and national insurance) The Home Office (visa and passport applications) Contact details for Jeff can be found here . Within the Help & Advice page, you can find further information using the below buttons: Emergency Support Finances, Social Security & Employment Housing Immigration & Asylum Local Councillors Manchester City Council Useful Links Share this: Share on X (Opens in new window) X Share on Facebook (Opens in new window) Facebook Like Loading... Acas Helpline: 0300 123 1100 | Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Citizens Advice Bureau (CAB) Citizens Advice is an independent organisation specialising in confidential information and advice to assist people with legal, debt, consumer, housing and other problems in the United Kingdom. Citizens Advice Manchester : http://www.citizensadvicemanchester.org.uk/ 0808 278 7800 | Monday-Friday 09.30am-5pm Albert House 17 Bloom St Manchester M1 3HZ Department for Work and Pensions The Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) is responsible for welfare, pensions and child maintenance policy. As the UK’s biggest public service department, it administers the State Pension and a range of working age, disability and ill health benefits to around 20 million claimants and customers. Universal Credit : Sign in to your Universal Credit account – GOV.UK (www.gov.uk) 0800 328 5644 | Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Didsbury Jobcentre Plus Please be aware that all enquiries for Universal Credit should be made directly through your Universal Credit journal or by calling 0800 328 5644. Personal Independence Payment: Phone: 0800 121 4433 | Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Personal Independence Payment Post Handling Site B Wolverhampton WV99 1AH Disability Living Allowance: If you were born after 8 April 1948: 0800 121 4600 | Monday-Friday 9am-5pm If you were born on or before 8 April 1948: 0800 731 0122 | Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Department for Work & Pensions Disability Living Allowance Warbreck House Warbreck Hill Road Blackpool FY2 0YE Attendance Allowance: Phone: 0800 731 0122 | Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Freepost DWP Attendance Allowance Making a complaint: If you would like to make a complaint about the DWP, please use the following link to find further information on the DWP complaints procedure: Complaints procedure – Department for Work and Pensions – GOV.UK (www.gov.uk) HMRC HMRC is the UK’s tax, payments and customs authority, and collects the money that pays for the UK’s public services and help families and individuals with targeted financial support. Amongst other things, HMRC is responsible for Child Benefit, National Insurance and other tax-related measures. You can find out how to contact them by visiting the website here . headings: h2: WORKING HARD FOR MANCHESTER WITHINGTON
h2: CONTACT JEFF
pages.text_excerpt: Contact – JEFF SMITH MP JEFF SMITH MP WORKING HARD FOR MANCHESTER WITHINGTON Menu Skip to content Home About Parliament PMQs & Parliamentary Tours Drug Law Reform Mental Health Environment Policies Assisted Dying Bill Israel, Palestine & Lebanon Help & Advice Cost of Living Crisis Cost of Living Crisis Energy Support Debt Advice Mortgage repayments Emergency Support Finances, Social Security & Employment Housing Immigration & Asylum Local Councillors Contact Privacy Policy Your Local Election Candidates for May 2026 Cllr Suzannah Reeves – Old Moat Ward Ben Williams – Didsbury West Ward Cllr Joanna Midgley – Chorlton Park Ward Cllr John Hacking – Chorlton Ward Cllr Andrew Simcock – Didsbury East Ward Cllr Angela Gartside – Withington Ward Contact There is a strict parliamentary protocol which prevents MPs from intervening on behalf of another MP’s constituents. If it is the first time you have contacted Jeff, you will need to provide your full name and postal address, as well as e-mail address and a phone number. To find out if Jeff is your MP, please click here and enter your postcode. Before getting in contact, you may be able to find the information on the Help & Advice page useful to you, including the role of an MP and the appropriate point of contact for your concerns. How To Get In Touch With Me Please do get in touch if you’ve got a problem – or you’re from either a local organisation, a community group or business, and you think we should meet – by emailing me at jeff.smith.mp@parliament.uk or calling on 0161 445 0678. You can also use the contact form below . Please note that we will be unable to respond without any contact details provided so leave your name, e-mail and full address. ← Back Message sent Thank you for your message to Jeff Smith MP. Your email has been received. HAVE YOU INCLUDED YOUR ADDRESS DETAILS? If this is the first time you have contacted me, please be aware there is a strict parliamentary convention that MPs can only deal with matters raised by their own constituents or businesses in the constituency. If you are a Manchester Withington constituent, please ensure that you have provided the following information: · Full name · Postal address · E-mail · Contact telephone number (where possible) Members of Parliament are there to help with matters for which Parliament or central government is responsible. For example, this might be Home Office applications, HMRC tax issues, DWP benefits & pensions; or queries about government policy. If you are unsure who your local MP is, you can find out here http://findyourmp.parliament.uk/ For some matters that are more local, the appropriate first step may be to contact your local councillor. You can find out who that is here https://jeffsmithmp.com/local-councillors/ I try to reply to emails as quickly as possible but please note I receive a large number of emails, letters and phone calls every day, so I would ask for your understanding if you do not receive an immediate reply. If you copy me into an email to someone else, its content will be noted but further action may not be taken. Very rarely I may miss an email, so if you haven’t received a response after two weeks, please do not hesitate to get back in touch. When I take up casework on your behalf, it may be necessary for me or my office to share personal details that you provide such as your telephone number and email address, with government departments, local authorities, public bodies and other organisations. I will only share as much information as is necessary to progress your case. You can find out more information about how I collect and use personal data here. https://jeffsmithmp.com/2018/04/01/privacy-notice/ Thank you once again for contacting me. Website: http://www.jeffsmithmp.com Twitter: @JeffSmithetc Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/jeffsmithforwithington/ Name (required) Email (required) Phone Address (required) Message Contact Us Submitting form Δ Appointments I and my team can meet

pages.title: Manchester MPs Campaign for Local Government Support – JEFF SMITH MP
pages.meta_description: I have joined other Manchester MPs in writing to the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, to ask that the Government delivers a fair and substantial uplift in funding for Manchester City Council as part of the upcoming Local Government Finance Settlement.
